steps.title: Rewrite as a quadratic and set up the discriminant
steps.content:
Shortcut (discriminant): Expand $-(3x-7)^2 = p + 17$ into standard form:
$-(9x^2 - 42x + 49) = p + 17$
$9x^2 - 42x + (66 + p) = 0$
For no real solution, the discriminant must be negative: $b^2 - 4ac \lt 0$.

steps.title: Solve the discriminant inequality for p
steps.content:
$(-42)^2 - 4(9)(66+p) \lt 0$
$1764 - 2376 - 36p \lt 0$
$-612 - 36p \lt 0$
$p \gt -17$
The least integer greater than $-17$ is $p = -16$. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
